relevant_techniques.name: Exploit Public-Facing Application
relevant_techniques.why: The new POST /v1/webhooks/stripe endpoint is internet-facing; injection + weak signature verification are directly reachable from the edge.

relevant_techniques.name: Exploitation for Credential Access
relevant_techniques.why: A timing side-channel on the HMAC compare lets an attacker recover/forge a valid signature without the secret being transmitted.

relevant_techniques.name: Unsecured Credentials: Credentials In Files
relevant_techniques.why: A signing secret committed to the repo is exactly the artifact this technique describes; pairs with the timing flaw to forge signatures outright.

relevant_techniques.name: Endpoint Denial of Service: Application Exhaustion
relevant_techniques.why: A memory-safety crash in the header parser on the request path is a remotely-triggerable availability hit.

advisories.title: Algorithm confusion (key type) in golang-jwt
advisories.note: Not in CISA KEV, but reachable here because the webhook path validates a service token with the vulnerable verifier; PoC public.

navigator_layer_hint: Overlay these 4 techniques against blue-team Sigma/YARA coverage to read the residual detection gap (analyzing-apt-group-with-mitre-navigator multi-layer analysis).
id_provenance: T1190 appears verbatim in the source skills (executing-red-team-exercise, exploiting-sql-injection-vulnerabilities, the CVSS/KEV/EPSS skills). T1552.001 and T1212 are the standard ATT&CK technique IDs for the Credential Access tactic (TA0006) that executing-red-team-exercise Step 1 enumerates ('Credential Access (TA0006): ... credential stuffing'); the skill names the tactic, the IDs are the canonical mapping. T1499.004 maps the fuzz-found DoS to ATT&CK Impact.
source: analyzing-apt-group-with-mitre-navigator (query ATT&CK data, multi-layer overlay) + performing-cve-prioritization-with-kev-catalog (KEV/EPSS advisory enrichment)
